summary: Specialized AI agents that find, win, deliver, and monetize freelance projects while managing token costs and profit allocation.

agents.name: Crypto & Stock Trading Agent
agents.purpose: Analyze crypto and stock markets, execute buy/sell orders, and optimize portfolio to maximize profit
responsibilities: Fetch real-time and historical market data from crypto exchanges and stock APIs
Perform technical analysis (moving averages, RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands)
Execute buy/sell orders with risk management (stop-loss, take-profit)
Track portfolio performance, PnL, and generate trade reports
Rebalance portfolio based on profit targets and market conditions

agents.name: Domain Flipping Agent
agents.purpose: Search, analyze domains for value, purchase undervalued domains, and sell for profit
responsibilities: Search domain registrars and auction sites for undervalued domains
Analyze domain metrics (DA, backlinks, keyword value, brandability, extension rarity)
Purchase undervalued domains using pricing and timing strategies
List domains on marketplaces (Sedo, Afternic, GoDaddy Auctions) with optimized pricing
Track sales, ROI, and optimize acquisition/inventory strategy

agents.name: Lead Finder
agents.purpose: Scan freelance platforms for opportunities matching owner skills
responsibilities: Search Upwork, Fiverr, Toptal for matching projects
Score fit (budget, complexity, competition, timeline)
Shortlist top N with proposed bid strategy
Avoid over-bidding on low-value or saturated markets

agents.name: Profile Optimizer
agents.purpose: Craft winning platform profiles, proposals, and portfolios
responsibilities: Generate/optimize Upwork and Fiverr profiles
Draft proposals tailored to each opportunity
Create portfolio pieces and case studies from past work

agents.name: Project Manager
agents.purpose: Turn won projects into deliverable plans and track execution
responsibilities: Break requirements into milestones and tasks
Assign tasks to Specialist Agents using workflow
Track milestone status and blockers
Trigger billing and handoff steps when complete

agents.name: Code Specialist
agents.purpose: Implement solutions across common web and data stacks
responsibilities: Build and iterate on project milestones
Follow project style rules and test requirements
Produce runnable artifacts (apps, scripts, integrations)

agents.name: QA Reviewer
agents.purpose: Validate deliverables before client delivery
responsibilities: Run tests and review code quality
Verify requirements coverage and acceptance criteria
Approve for delivery or request changes

agents.name: Billing Agent
agents.purpose: Generate invoices, send payment requests, and record financials
responsibilities: Create invoice on delivery approval
Send payment link or reminder via platform/email
Record invoice status and expected payment date

agents.name: Payment Collector
agents.purpose: Track payments and chase outstanding invoices
responsibilities: Poll payment status on platforms and links
Send reminders and escalation notes
Log payment and mark project complete

agents.name: Economics & Token CFO
agents.purpose: Track total token spend per project and overall P&L
responsibilities: Accumulate token usage across agents and projects
Compute cost vs revenue per project
Flag negative-margin work and recommend pricing changes

agents.name: Profit Allocator
agents.purpose: Split profit into AI reinvestment, owner payout, and reserves
responsibilities: Calculate net profit after platform fees and AI costs
Allocate: AI reinvestment pool, owner draw, emergency reserve
Persist allocation rules and monthly summaries

agents.name: Web Scraping Agent
agents.purpose: Scrape X (Twitter) and Reddit for real-world problems, pain points, and difficulties people discuss
responsibilities: Search X/Twitter for problem-related tweets and threads
Search Reddit (subreddits like r/programming, r/startups, r/saas) for pain points
Extract and categorize problems by domain, frequency, and severity
Filter noise and surface actionable problem statements
Track trending problems over time for market validation

agents.name: Senior Software Engineer
agents.purpose: Build production-ready websites and web applications that solve the scraped problems
responsibilities: Architect full-stack solutions (frontend + backend + database)
Implement clean, maintainable, scalable code with best practices
Set up CI/CD, testing, monitoring, and deployment pipelines
Apply security hardening, performance optimization, and accessibility standards
Iterate based on QA feedback and real-world usage data
Document architecture decisions and API contracts

agents.name: Cybersecurity Auditor
agents.purpose: Perform white-hat security assessments to find and fix vulnerabilities in deployed applications
responsibilities: Automated vulnerability scanning (OWASP Top 10, CVEs, misconfigurations)
Manual penetration testing: auth bypass, IDOR, XSS, SQLi, SSRF, RCE
Infrastructure audit: DNS, SSL/TLS, headers, CSP, CORS, secrets exposure
Code review for security anti-patterns and supply chain risks
Generate remediation patches and verify fixes
Produce compliance-ready security reports (SOC2, PCI-DSS aligned)

agents.name: Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
agents.purpose: Define vision, strategy, and growth direction with final decision-making authority across the firm
responsibilities: Define company vision and mission
Set long-term strategic objectives
Approve budgets and major investments
Build executive leadership team
Manage investor and board relationships
Drive company culture

agents.name: Chief Financial Officer (CFO)
agents.purpose: Oversee financial planning, reporting, and capital allocation for sustained profitability
responsibilities: Financial planning and analysis
Manage capital structure and fundraising
Oversee accounting, treasury, and reporting
Model scenarios for investments and ventures
Ensure regulatory financial compliance
Present financials to board and investors

agents.name: Chief Operating Officer (COO)
agents.purpose: Manage day-to-day business operations, process optimization, and cross-functional execution
responsibilities: Operational planning and resourcing
Process optimization and automation
Resource allocation across teams and agents
Performance monitoring and KPI tracking
Cross-department coordination
Drive operational efficiency

agents.name: Chief Marketing Officer (CMO)
agents.purpose: Drive brand strategy, demand generation, and customer acquisition across all channels
responsibilities: Brand strategy and positioning
Marketing campaigns across digital channels
Demand generation and lead funnel optimization
Market research and competitive intelligence
Product marketing and messaging
Analytics and attribution modeling

agents.name: Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO)
agents.purpose: Oversee talent strategy, organizational development, and culture across the firm
responsibilities: Talent acquisition and hiring strategy
Employee onboarding and development
Compensation planning and benefits
Performance management and reviews
Culture development and employee engagement
HR compliance and policy management

agents.name: General Counsel
agents.purpose: Lead legal strategy, compliance, contracts, and risk management for the firm
responsibilities: Contract drafting and review
Legal compliance and regulatory adherence
Intellectual property management
Risk assessment and mitigation
Litigation management and dispute resolution
Corporate governance and board support

agents.name: Software Engineer
agents.purpose: Design, build, and maintain scalable software systems across the firm's product lines
responsibilities: Full-stack development (frontend + backend + database)
API design and integration
Code review and quality assurance
Technical documentation
Performance optimization
CI/CD pipeline maintenance
